channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Mamelodi Sundowns take 3-0 first-leg lead over Stade Malien in CAF Champions League quarterfinal
item.description: Mamelodi Sundowns delivered a commanding 3-0 victory over Stade Malien in the first leg of the CAF Champions League quarterfinals at Loftus Versfeld Stadium in Pretoria. Goals from Khuliso Mudau, Bray...

item.title: Kenya hosts World Rally Championship Safari Rally in Naivasha
item.description: Kenya is buzzing with excitement as the third leg of the 2026 World Rally Championship (WRC) takes place in Naivasha from March 12 to 15. The iconic Safari Rally features a total distance of 842.9 km,...

item.title: Igbinedion Education Centre Expels Students Over Brutal Bullying Incident in Benin City
item.description: Some students of Igbinedion Education Centre for Secondary School for Benin City don dey expelled because dem assault and bully one of their fellow student inside classroom. One video wey go viral sho...

item.title: Senator Bato dela Rosa’s request to use Senate Mansion denied due to repairs
item.description: Senator Ronald “Bato” dela Rosa requested permission from Senate President Vicente “Tito” Sotto III to use the Senate Mansion in Baguio City for his family. Sotto confirmed receiving a letter from Del...

item.title: Australian RAAF Wedgetail Deployment to Middle East Prompts Strategic Discussion
item.description: The deployment of an RAAF E-7A Wedgetail aircraft to the UAE has sparked debate over Australia's role in Middle East operations. While officially framed as a defensive mission requested by the UAE, ex...

item.title: Prisoner orchestrates plot to disrupt £45m drug smuggling trial
item.description: William Todd, 61, a convicted murderer with a history of a dramatic prison escape, has been sentenced to seven years for conspiracy to pervert the course of justice. Todd orchestrated a complex scheme...

item.title: Ondo LCDA Secretary Released After Ransom Payment Following Five-Day Kidnapping
item.description: Hon. Joseph Kayode Aladesuyi, the Secretary of Okeluju Local Council Development Area (LCDA) in Akoko North-West Local Government Area of Ondo State, has been released by his kidnappers after spending...

item.title: Abu Dhabi police detain 45 for filming locations and sharing misinformation amid Iran strikes
item.description: Abu Dhabi Police have detained 45 individuals, including foreign nationals, amid the ongoing tensions due to Iran strikes in the Gulf region. The arrests were made by the Criminal Investigation Depart...

item.title: Court allows Ruto’s 21 presidential advisers to resume duties
item.description: President William Ruto’s team of 21 presidential advisers has resumed work after a High Court decision lifted a previous suspension. The court had earlier declared their appointments unconstitutional ...

item.title: Anthropic Updates Claude AI to Generate Interactive Charts and Diagrams Inline in Conversations
item.description: Anthropic has rolled out a significant update to its Claude AI chatbot, enabling it to create and display custom interactive visualizations such as charts, diagrams, graphs, and other graphical elemen...

item.title: United States announces reward for information on Iran’s Supreme Leader and top military officials
item.description: The United States government has announced a reward of up to $10 million for credible information regarding several senior Iranian officials, including the newly appointed Supreme Leader Ayatollah Moj...

item.title: Parliamentary Committee Welcomes Swift SANDF Deployment to Combat Crime in Affected Communities
item.description: The Select Committee on Security and Justice in South Africa's Parliament has expressed appreciation for the rapid deployment of the South African National Defence Force (SANDF) to various communities...

item.title: Supreme Court Observes Potential Impact of Mandatory Menstrual Leave on Employment Opportunities for Women
item.description: The Supreme Court of India expressed concern over repeated Public Interest Litigations (PILs) seeking mandatory paid menstrual leave of two days per month for female workers. A bench led by Chief Just...

item.title: Ombudsman clarifies photo in ex-Marines' affidavit pertains to Velasco's term
item.description: Ombudsman Jesus Crispin Remulla clarified on March 13, 2026, that one of the photos cited in the affidavit of 18 individuals claiming to be former Marines depicted a delivery of money to a lawmaker du...

item.title: US-led War on Iran Escalates Without Achieving Its Objectives
item.description: Two weeks into the US-led military campaign against Iran, the situation has deteriorated rather than improved, according to political commentator Jonathan Freedland. While proponents claim the war aim...

item.title: Veteran Tanzanian Banker and Leader Idris Rashidi Passes Away at 78
item.description: Idris Rashidi, the fourth governor of the Bank of Tanzania, has passed away at the age of 78 on the night of Thursday, March 12, 2026, after battling cancer. He was born in 1948 in Mbulu District, Man...

item.title: Wolfgang Puck reveals 2026 Oscars Governors Ball menu and addresses Hollywood's weight-loss trends
item.description: Celebrity chef Wolfgang Puck, who has catered the Governors Ball – the official afterparty for the Academy Awards – for the 32nd consecutive year, shared details of the 2026 menu with the Daily Mail. ...

item.title: Insights from Bob Winnel on Tackling Canberra’s Housing Shortage
item.description: Bob Winnel, founder of Village Building Co, is stepping down after nearly 50 years in the construction and property development industry. Known for his commitment to affordable housing, Winnel has con...

item.title: Mother of Injured Tumbler Ridge Girl Overwhelmed by Mail Support Requests a Pause
item.description: The mother of 12-year-old Maya Gebala, who survived the Tumbler Ridge school shooting in British Columbia, has asked the public to pause sending letters and packages to their family. Cia Edmonds share...

item.title: Ennerdale Residents Clash with Police During Shack Demolition Operation
item.description: On Thursday in Ennerdale, south of Johannesburg, an operation to demolish unoccupied shacks escalated into a violent confrontation. Johannesburg Metro Police officers from Region G and the by-law mana...
